UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents an authenticated business location or user managing the app. This entity links all operational data to a specific local instance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity (Firebase UID).

email.description: The email address associated with this user profile.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an item available for sale in the Point of Sale (POS) system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

price.description: The selling price of the product.

StockItem.title: StockItem
StockItem.description: Represents an inventory item or raw ingredient used in the business operations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StockItem entity.

name.description: The name of the stock item (e.g., 'TOMATE', 'PAN').

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the stock item (e.g., 'KG', 'UNID', 'LITRO').

stock.description: The current quantity of the item in stock.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer order placed through the POS system, to be prepared by the kitchen.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

clientName.description: The name of the customer for this order.

items.description: A list of products included in this order with their quantities and prices at the time of sale.

callerNumber.description: A random number assigned to the order for display in the kitchen monitor.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the order was created.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records individual financial movements (income or expense) within a specific shift or day.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

concept.description: A description of the transaction (e.g., 'VENTA: JUAN PEREZ', 'COMPRA PAN').

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction.

type.description: Indicates if the transaction is an 'ingreso' (income) or 'egreso' (expense).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the transaction occurred.

Closure.title: Closure
Closure.description: Represents a summary of financial activity for a closed shift or business day.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Closure entity.

date.description: The date and time when the shift or business day was officially closed.

cashAmount.description: The total amount of cash handled (income minus expenses) during the closed period.

transferAmount.description: The total amount of funds processed via electronic transfer during the closed period.

totalAmount.description: The grand total of all financial movements (cash + transfer) for the closed period.

transactionCount.description: The total number of individual transactions included in this closure summary.

definition.description: Stores the profile data for an authenticated business location/user. This document's ID corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. It serves as the root for all other user-specific data.

params.name: appId
params.description: The static unique identifier for this application instance or tenant.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user, which is also the ID of the UserProfile document.

definition.description: Contains product listings for a specific business location. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: appId
params.description: The static unique identifier for this application instance or tenant.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the owner of this product.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product document.

definition.description: Manages inventory items and raw ingredients for a specific business location. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: appId
params.description: The static unique identifier for this application instance or tenant.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the owner of this stock item.

params.name: stockItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the stock item document.

definition.description: Stores customer orders placed at a specific business location. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: appId
params.description: The static unique identifier for this application instance or tenant.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the business location that owns this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order document.

definition.description: Records individual financial movements (income/expense) for a specific business location. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: appId
params.description: The static unique identifier for this application instance or tenant.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the business location that owns this transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction document.

definition.description: Contains summary reports of financial activity for closed shifts or business days at a specific location. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: appId
params.description: The static unique identifier for this application instance or tenant.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the business location that owns this closure report.

params.name: closureId
params.description: The unique identifier for the closure document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to enforce strict user-based data segregation and authorization independence, adhering to the core design principles. All data pertaining to a specific business location (represented by a UserProfile) is nested under a path that includes a static application ID and the user's unique Firebase UID. This pattern is: `/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/<collection_name>/{documentId}`.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This structure achieves authorization independence by embedding the `userId` directly into the Firestore path for all user-specific collections. For example, a Product document is stored at `/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/products/{productId}`. When a user attempts to read or write to this path, Firestore security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This eliminates the need for any `get()` operations in security rules to fetch parent documents or denormalized `userId` fields for authorization, thereby preventing complex hierarchical authorization dependencies and enabling atomic operations (transactions/batches) to succeed without rule failures related to missing parent data. Each document in the subcollections (Products, StockItems, Orders, Transactions, Closures) also explicitly stores a `userId` field, which acts as a denormalized reference to the owner. This redundancy within the document, combined with its presence in the path, reinforces ownership and simplifies rule logic by allowing a direct check: `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` AND `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This design inherently supports Query and Access Patterns (QAPs) for secure `list` operations. Because all user-specific data is strictly partitioned by `userId` in the path, any `list` query issued by a user is implicitly scoped to their own data. Security rules will grant read access only if `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` in the collection path being queried. This ensures that users can only retrieve a list of documents that they are authorized to access, preventing data leakage and eliminating the need for client-side filtering or complex server-side filtering logic within security rules, thus optimizing performance and simplifying rule definitions.
